items.verseEn: You are not, [O Muḥammad], by the favor of your Lord, a madman.

items.verse: بَلْ قَالُوٓا۟ أَضْغَـٰثُ أَحْلَـٰمٍۭ بَلِ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ بَلْ هُوَ شَاعِرٌۭ فَلْيَأْتِنَا بِـَٔايَةٍۢ كَمَآ أُرْسِلَ ٱلْأَوَّلُونَ
items.verseEn: But they say, "[The revelation is but] a mixture of false dreams; rather, he has invented it; rather, he is a poet. So let him bring us a sign just as the previous [messengers] were sent [with miracles]."

items.verse: فَتَوَلَّىٰ بِرُكْنِهِۦ وَقَالَ سَـٰحِرٌ أَوْ مَجْنُونٌۭ
items.verseEn: But he turned away with his supporters and said, "A magician or a madman."

items.verse: وَلَا بِقَوْلِ كَاهِنٍۢ ۚ قَلِيلًۭا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ
items.verseEn: Nor the word of a soothsayer; little do you remember.

categories.name: مُسَمَّيَاتٌ وُصِفَتْ بِكَرِيمٍ
categories.nameEn: Things Described as Karim (Noble) in the Quran

items.verseEn: Indeed, it is a noble Qur’ān.

items.verse: أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّۭا ۚ لَّهُمْ دَرَجَـٰتٌ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَمَغْفِرَةٌۭ وَرِزْقٌۭ كَرِيمٌۭ
items.verseEn: Those are the believers, truly. For them are degrees [of high position] with their Lord and forgiveness and noble provision.

items.verse: إِنَّمَا تُنذِرُ مَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَخَشِىَ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنَ بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۖ فَبَشِّرْهُ بِمَغْفِرَةٍۢ وَأَجْرٍۢ كَرِيمٍ
items.verseEn: You can only warn one who follows the message and fears the Most Merciful unseen. So give him good tidings of forgiveness and noble reward.

items.verse: يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِن تَنصُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ يَنصُرْكُمْ وَيُثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَكُمْ
items.verseEn: O you who have believed, if you support Allāh, He will support you and plant firmly your feet.

items.verse: وَلَوْ عَلِمَ ٱللَّهُ فِيهِمْ خَيْرًۭا لَّأَسْمَعَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ أَسْمَعَهُمْ لَتَوَلَّوا۟ وَّهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ
items.verseEn: Had Allāh known any good in them, He would have made them hear. And if He had made them hear, they would [still] have turned away, while they were refusing.

items.verse: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمْ لَا تُفْسِدُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّمَا نَحْنُ مُصْلِحُونَ
items.verseEn: And when it is said to them, "Do not cause corruption on the earth," they say, "We are but reformers."

items.verse: ثُمَّ تَوَلَّيْتُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ فَلَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ وَرَحْمَتُهُۥ لَكُنتُم مِّنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ
items.verseEn: Then you turned away after that. And if not for the favor of Allāh upon you and His mercy, you would have been among the losers.

categories.name: أَحْوَالُ الْوُجُوهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ
categories.nameEn: Descriptions of Faces on the Day of Judgement

items.verseEn: [Some] faces, that Day, will be humbled,
[Other] faces, that Day, will show pleasure.
[Some] faces, that Day, will be radiant,
[Some] faces, that Day, will be bright -

harf.hint: Divine forgiveness — the merciful pardon that erases past sins, promised to those who believe and do good deeds
harf.arabicVerse: وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ ۙ لَهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌۭ وَأَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌۭ
harf.verse: 5:9 - Allāh has promised those who believe and do righteous deeds [that] for them there is forgiveness and great reward.

deduction.title: The Soul Behind the Idol
deduction.intro: In the shadow of a great exodus from tyranny, this mysterious figure waited in the wilderness. While the leader of a chosen people ascended a sacred mount for divine communion, a great trial descended upon those left behind. This individual seized the moment to reshape the beliefs of an entire community. When the leader returned with truth, a unique and eternal punishment was decreed. Can you unravel who this controversial and cunning soul was?
clues: I traveled with a people who had been freed from the grip of a powerful oppressor through miraculous signs.
When their leader went up to the mountain to receive guidance, I waited among those who remained behind.
I gathered precious metal from their ornaments and fashioned it into something that produced a sound.
Through this creation, I convinced an entire generation that they had found their god in the wilderness.
When the leader returned, I was singled out and asked what drove me to such deception.
My punishment in this world was that no one would touch me or come near, a mark of shame I bear forever.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: The Samiri
Prophet Musa
Pharaoh's magicians
Haman, minister of Pharaoh
Iblis, the rejected one

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: The making of the golden calf
The confrontation with Pharaoh
The construction of a great ark
The refusal to bow before Adam
The destruction of the cities of Lut
trial.answer: The making of the golden calf

location.label: Key Element
options: A golden calf idol
A staff that turned into a serpent
A she-camel that emerged from rock
An ark built upon a mountain
A throne carried upon the wind

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Condemned to be untouchable, forever saying 'no contact'
Drowned alongside the tyrant and his army
Cast into a fire that became cool and peaceful
Struck with blindness for refusing truth
Abandoned in a pit by jealous kin
outcome.answer: Condemned to be untouchable, forever saying 'no contact'

deduction.arabic: قَالَ فَٱذْهَبْ فَإِنَّ لَكَ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ أَن تَقُولَ لَا مِسَاسَ ۖ وَإِنَّ لَكَ مَوْعِدًۭا لَّن تُخْلَفَهُۥ ۖ وَٱنظُرْ إِلَىٰٓ إِلَـٰهِكَ ٱلَّذِى ظَلْتَ عَلَيْهِ عَاكِفًۭا ۖ لَّنُحَرِّقَنَّهُۥ ثُمَّ لَنَنسِفَنَّهُۥ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ نَسْفًا
deduction.verse: [Moses] said, "Then go. And indeed, it is [decreed] for you in [this] life to say, 'No contact.' And indeed, you have an appointment [in the Hereafter] you will not fail to keep. And look at your 'god' to which you remained devoted. We will surely burn it and blow it [i.e., its ashes] into the sea with a blast. (20:97)

scramble.hint: A beloved, oft-repeated supplication asking Allah for goodness in both this life and the Hereafter, and protection from Hellfire — the du'a of those who hope for Allah's mercy.
scramble.arabic: وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَقُولُ رَبَّنَآ ءَاتِنَا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةًۭ وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ حَسَنَةًۭ وَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ
scramble.verseEn: But among them is he who says, "Our Lord, give us in this world [that which is] good and in the Hereafter [that which is] good and protect us from the punishment of the Fire."
